Article Title: BJP summons top Karnataka netas over cross-voting
Article Text:
BY Vijayendra, LoP R Ashoka

Bengaluru: BJP brass has summoned Karnataka state chief BY Vijayendra, LoP R Ashoka and national general secretary Radhamohan Das Agarwal to Delhi on June 23 to explain cross-voting by NDA legislators in Thursday's legislative council elections. Congress won five of seven seats despite NDA having 82 MLAs in the assembly.BJP's two candidates polled 56 votes against the party's strength of 64 MLAs, while ally JDS, which has 18 MLAs, got 14 votes for its candidate despite being allotted three BJP votes under the NDA arrangement. Together, the two parties estimate that 11 legislators voted against the alliance's strategy.Party insiders said the BJP brass was upset over the state unit's failure to keep its flock together and ensure coordination with JDS. Atate functionaries are expected to explain the setback and seek action against the "black sheep".Defending the alliance's electoral plan, Vijayendra said BJP and JDS had jointly decided to contest aggressively for a third seat. "HD Kumaraswamy suggested we should not give up the contest. Based on discussions with our leadership, we backed the JDS candidate and fought together. During voting, six to seven cross-votes happened from JDS side and four to five from BJP side.We have some information on who played this game," he said.Vijayendra said he had sought an appointment with BJP president Nitin Nabin to brief him. "The party brass will take this seriously and whatever action is required will be taken," he said. The state unit has constituted a committee to probe the cross-voting and submit a report by June 25.
Article Summary: BJP brass summons Karnataka state chief BY Vijayendra, LoP R Ashoka and national general secretary Radhamohan Das Agarwal. They will explain cross-voting by NDA legislators in legislative council elections. Congress won five of seven seats despite NDA having 82 MLAs in the assembly.

Article Title: Delhi woman mistaken for child-lifter, stripped
Article Text:
Delhi woman mistaken for child-lifter, stripped

KORAPUT: Odisha Police Thursday arrested 16 people who were part of a mob that chased and assaulted a woman from Delhi and her male colleague from Gujarat, mistaking them for child kidnappers, in a remote village in Rayagada district on June 16. The accused also half-stripped the woman.The two work with voluntary organisations under fellowship programmes. While the woman is engaged in solid waste management initiatives in Kalahandi district, the man from Surat is associated with a turmeric packaging project in Kandhamal district.According to police, they were travelling from Daringbadi in Kandhamal to Thuamul Rampur in Kalahandi district on a scooter. They stopped near Kandulguda village to seek directions.As the two interacted with a group of children and gave them chocolates, it aroused the suspicion of some villagers, who quickly spread a rumour that the outsiders were child-lifters. The two development workers alleged they were attacked despite repeatedly showing their identity cards and explaining the purpose of their visit.Police have registered four cases, with preliminary investigations suggesting 40 to 50 could be involved in the attack. Rayagada SP Raj Prasad said, "Kalyansinghpur police rescued the two victims and arrested 16 persons based on CCTV footage. We are verifying videos recorded by people at the spot."
Article Summary: A woman from Delhi and her male colleague from Gujarat were attacked. The two work with voluntary organisations under fellowship programmes. The accused also half-stripped the woman. Police have registered four cases, with preliminary investigations suggesting 40 to 50 could be involved in the attack.

Article Title: 'Armed with weapons': TMC claims 'possible murder' attempt by BJP on Abhishek Banerjee at Kolkata airport
Article Text:
Abhishek Banerjee

NEW DELHI: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) on Friday alleged that a BJP supporter carrying weapons attempted to target the party's national general secretary, Abhishek Banerjee , at Kolkata airport, describing the incident as a possible attempt on his life.In a post on social media, the party claimed that a “BJP Bengal goon armed with weapons” had possibly planned to “murder” Banerjee. The TMC also accused the BJP-led state administration of failing to maintain law and order and allowing political violence to flourish.“A BJP Bengal goon ARMED WITH WEAPONS possibly planned to MURDER our Hon'ble National General Secretary Shri Abhishek Banerjee,” the party wrote. It further alleged that criminals were operating with “absolute impunity” and questioned whether the episode amounted to a “state-sponsored attempt to eliminate a political opponent”.The TMC has repeatedly accused the BJP of encouraging attacks on its leaders, while the BJP has argued that such incidents reflect public anger against the former ruling party.Abhishek Banerjee himself was among the first senior TMC leaders to face hostility after the election results. Days after the defeat, eggs were hurled at him during a public appearance. Senior TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee was similarly targeted shortly afterwards.The latest allegation also follows another incident involving senior TMC leader and former minister Udayan Guha. On Thursday, eggs were thrown at Guha outside a court in Dinhata, a day after his arrest in an alleged extortion case. Television visuals showed Guha, wearing a helmet and escorted by police officers carrying shields, when an egg was thrown in his direction. The projectile struck his helmet.
Article Summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) on Friday alleged that a BJP supporter carrying weapons attempted to target the party's national general secretary, Abhishek Banerjee. The TMC also accused the BJP-led state administration of failing to maintain law and order and allowing political violence to flourish.

Article Title: Bengal cops start probe into TMC's bank accounts
Article Text:
Bengal cops start probe into TMC's bank accounts

KOLKATA: A dissident MLA's complaint alleging substantial unaccounted deposits in Trinamool Congress 's name led Bengal Police to open a probe Friday into the party's bank accounts under penal sections pertaining to organised crime, cheating and criminal conspiracy. Bidhannagar police commissionerate's cybercrime wing registered the case under BNS and Information Technology Act after legislator Biswanath Das sought an investigation into the source and movement of funds through three accounts in TMC's name with HDFC Bank's Central Plaza branch in Kolkata.The probe was ordered a day after the news broke that former minister Aroop Biswas had written to HDFC Bank a week ago asking for the party's accounts to be frozen. Biswas, who signed TMC's audit report to Election Commission in his role as treasurer, was removed from the post June 5.According to the report, the three accounts hold Rs 535 crore, which constitutes over half of the party's total declared funds of Rs 1,018.8 crore, as on March 31, 2025. Das alleged funds generated through illegal activities, had been channelled through electronic banking platforms before being deposited."The MLA has asked police to investigate whether digital communication systems and online banking mechanisms had been used to conceal the movement of funds. He also urged us to preserve banking records, KYC documents and electronic transaction data," a senior police officer said.
Article Summary: Bidhannagar police commissionerate's cybercrime wing registered the case. Legislator Biswanath Das sought an investigation into the source and movement of funds through three accounts in TMC's name with HDFC Bank's Central Plaza branch in Kolkata.

Article Title: 'Hope Speaker gives us justice': Abhishek Banerjee files disqualification pleas against rebel MPs
Article Text:
Abhishek Banerjee meeting Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress (TMC) leader Abhishek Banerjee Friday met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla to submit a disqualification petitions against party MPs who have sought to merge with the lesser-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI).Banerjee, who is former West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee's nephew, said he submitted around 20 petitions involving relevant judgements and media reports."I can only present my side before him, and I have done that. I submitted the disqualification petitions that I showed you. Around 20 such petitions have been submitted. All our prayers are included in these petitions, and all relevant judgments have been cited. Each petition is 21 pages long. We have also attached all the media reports that came to our notice and submitted them along with the petitions", he told news agency PTI after the meeting.Expressing hope that Lok Sabha Om Birla would "act in accordance with the Constitution," Banerjee said: "The Lok Sabha Speaker is the custodian of the House, not the protector of the government of the day. Every citizen, especially elected representatives, must work within the ambit of the law.Therefore, one has to function according to the provisions laid down in the Constitution."In a dramatic development, 20 rebel Trinamool Congress MPs informed Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla that their group has merged with NCPI, which is registered in West Bengal’s Howrah and had contested a few seats in the 2023 Tripura assembly elections, and that they will be backing the BJP-led NDA.Incidentally, “to save your rights, reject political turncoats” was one of the slogans of NCPI. The merger will make the little-known party the second-biggest bloc (20 Lok Sabha members) in the governing alliance, after BJP (240), and ahead of TDP (16) and JDU (12). The rebels asked Birla to allot them seats with the treasury benches, as they were seated with opposition parties in Parliament till now as members of TMC.Sudip Bandyopadhyay, who as a sixth-term MP is the most experienced member of the breakaway bloc, also left open the possibility of going to Election Commission to stake claim as “real TMC”.After the meeting with Speaker Om Birla, Sudip Bandyopadhyay said the merger was guided by demands of the anti-defection law (Tenth Schedule of Constitution).The law does not recognise a split, a point stressed by Supreme Court in its 2022 judgment in the case of division in Shiv Sena as well, but makes an exception for merger of two-thirds of members of one party with another party. With 20 MPs, the TMC dissidents have one MP more than the required two-thirds figure as TMC has 28 members in Lok Sabha.NCPI is a registered unrecognised party with EC, one of 2,049 that have not been able to cross the threshold of poll performance needed to get recognised.The rebel MPs earlier met at the residence of Union minister Bhupender Yadav and were joined by BJP MP Nishikant Dubey, who played a crucial role in breaking TMC.Sources said 19 MPs were physically present while one member has pledged her support, indicating that more TMC Rajya Sabha members may resign in the coming days. Three have quit so far.
Article Summary: Abhishek Banerjee meets Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla to submit disqualification petitions against party MPs. TMC MPs have sought to merge with lesser-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India. The merger will make the little-known party the second-biggest bloc (20 Lok Sabha members) in the governing alliance.

Article Title: ‘Submit written explanation within 24 hours’: Shiv Sena (UBT) issues show-cause notice to absentee MPs, threatens disqualification
Article Text:
(With agency inputs)





MUMBAI: Shiv Sena (UBT) on Saturday has issued a fresh show-cause notice to its absentee MPs, threatening disqualification.The party’s Chief Whip in the Lok Sabha, Anil Desai, has issued formal show-cause notices to the MPs concerned. They have been given a strict 24-hour deadline to submit a written explanation for their conduct.The notice carries a stern warning. If the MPs fail to respond within the stipulated time, the party will treat it as voluntary relinquishment of membership. Subsequently, they will face action under the Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution (Anti-Defection Law).Amid ‘Operation Tiger’, a mass defection of UBT parliamentarians to the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena has pushed the party to the brink, exposing open hostility, absentee MPs, and threats of legal action.The crisis escalated on Thursday in New Delhi when a mandatory parliamentary party meeting called by the leadership witnessed a stark display of internal divisions.Out of the party’s nine Lok Sabha MPs, only three—Arvind Sawant, Anil Desai, and Rajabhau Waje—attended the meeting convened by the party whips. The remaining six—Nagesh Aashtikar, Sanjay Deshmukh, Sanjay Jadhav, Sanjay Dina Patil, Omprakash Rajenimbalkar, and Bhausaheb Wakchaure—remained absent.In response, the UBT faction moved swiftly to assert its authority. Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Raut announced that show-cause notices had already been issued to the six absentee MPs, initiating the formal process that could lead to their disqualification from the Lok Sabha."The process for taking action has started," Raut declared during a press conference. "We will make every effort to get them disqualified. If the Speaker of the Lok Sabha works according to the rules, the law, and the Supreme Court's directives, these people will be disqualified."The buzz surrounding “Operation Tiger” gained momentum after Shiv Sena MLC Chandrakant Raghuvanshi claimed that six MPs from Shiv Sena (UBT) had expressed confidence in Chief Minister Eknath Shinde and had already joined his faction.
Article Summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) on Saturday has issued a fresh show-cause notice to its absentee MPs, threatening disqualification. They have been given a strict 24-hour deadline to submit a written explanation for their conduct. If the MPs fail to respond within the stipulated time, the party will treat it as voluntary relinquishment of membership.

Article Title: Man from Navi Mumbai arrested for raping minor daughter for 10 years
Article Text:
Man arrested for allegedly sexually abusing his 16-year-old daughter since she was seven (Representative image)





NAVI MUMBAI: A 55-year-old man was arrested on Thursday for allegedly sexually abusing his 16-year-old daughter since 2016, when she was seven.Police said the girl gathered courage to lodge a complaint against her accused father as she claimed that she was unable to bear the repeated sexual abuse and threats for several years at their residence.API Pandurang Ghagare of Khandeshwar said the girl gathered courage to lodge a police complaint against her accused father as she claimed that she was unable to bear the repeated sexual abuse and threats for several years at their residence.Ghagare said, "The accused owns a hardware store in New Panvel. His wife has separated from him for the past five years due to frequent domestic quarrels. Hence, the girl, her elder brother and sister resided with their father. The girl did not reveal her sexual assaults until she became an adolescent after turning 16 years old.”The accused was booked under relevant sections of BNS for the offence of punishment for rape, aggravated rape, committing rape repeatedly on the same woman, committing rape on a girl under 16 years of age, voluntarily causing hurt and criminal intimidation along with the Pocso Act On Friday, the arrested accused was produced before Panvel district sessions court for the Pocso Act case and was remanded to police custody till Sunday. Thereafter, he will be sent to jail custody at Taloja Central Prison.
Article Summary: Man arrested for allegedly sexually abusing his 16-year-old daughter since she was seven. Police said the girl gathered courage to lodge a complaint against her accused father. His wife has separated from him for the past five years due to frequent domestic quarrels.

Article Title: MLC polls: Mahayuti eyes big gains in Marathwada
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

TOI





The elections to four Local Authorities’ Constituencies in Marathwada on Thursday witnessed an average of over 99% polling with Mahayuti leaders expressing hopes of massive wins due to claimed cross-voting from the MVA camp.Held under the shadow of ‘Operation Tiger’ aimed at poaching of Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs, the MLC polls allegedly saw confusion among a section of MVA voters associated with three MPs from Marathwada who are expected to ditch Uddhav Thackeray Among four constituencies that went for poll, Aurangabad-Jalna Local Authorities’ Constituency reported 98.9% polling whereas both Nanded Local Authorities’ Constituency and Parbhani-Hingoli Local Authorities’ Constituency registered 99.8% voting while the figure for Osmanabad-Latur-Beed Local Authorities’ Constituency was recorded at 99.5%. BJP Minister for OBC welfare Atul Save said the NDA will have a clean sweep in Marathwada and at the state-level in the MLC polls. “Mahayuti’s victory in Marathwada will be magnificent due to large-scale cross-voting from the MVA camp. Sena (UBT) MPs are joining the NDA to fulfill aspirations of development of their respective constituencies and such a switch will offer the Mahayuti early gains in MLC polls,” he toldCongress general secretary Jitendra Dehade said the voters will show opportunistic leaders their place in the upcoming elections.“The claims of cross-voting are baseless and we will give a tough fight,” he said.Senior Sena (UBT) leaders, however, refused to comment. Among major developments in the region, in Parbhani-Hingoli constituency, Congress had issued a whip asking its elected representatives to vote for Krushna Patil, the MVA candidate and son of Nagesh Patil Ashtikar, who is believed to be one of the poached MPs under the “Operation Tiger “.The official results for the MLC polls will be declared on June 22
Article Summary: The elections to four Local Authorities’ Constituencies in Marathwada on Thursday witnessed an average of over 99% polling. Mahayuti leaders expressed hopes of massive wins due to claimed cross-voting from the MVA camp. The official results for the MLC polls will be declared on June 22.

Article Title: Special CBI court’s verdict in Pawanraje Nimbalkar murder case on June 20
Article Text:
It is the CBI’s case that the murders were executed through a contract killing of Rs 25 lakh due to political rivalry.

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





A special CBI court in Mumbai will deliver its verdict on June 20 in the two-decade-old murder case of Dharashiv Congress leader Pawanraje Nimbalkar and his driver, Samad Kazi. Among the eight accused facing trial is former Maharashtra home minister and ex-NCP MP Padamsinh Patil, who is also Nimbalkar’s cousin.The case was listed for pronouncement of judgment on Tuesday, but the special judge said he would need another two to three days to complete the order.Nimbalkar and Kazi were shot dead on June 3, 2006, while travelling from Mumbai to Osmanabad (now Dharashiv). Two assailants intercepted their car at Kalamboli in Navi Mumbai and opened fire, killing both on the spot. The probe was initially conducted by the Navi Mumbai police before being transferred to the CBI following directions from the Bombay high court.Patil was arrested in June 2009 and granted bail in Sept the same year. The trial commenced in July 2011, during which the special court examined 128 witnesses.Nimbalkar’s son, Omprakash, is currently a Lok Sabha member from Shiv Sena (UBT). He was present in court on Tuesday, and said the families of the two victims had been waiting for justice for over 20 years.“We now only expect justice. An ordinary citizen has to struggle immensely to get justice,” he said.Omprakash said he had approached the HC seeking a CBI probe into the case, maintaining that the murders were politically motivated. He alleged that political rivalry had intensified after his father lost the 2004 assembly election by a narrow margin of 484 votes. “This was a pre-planned murder carried out to eliminate a political opponent,” he alleged.It is the CBI’s case that the murders were executed through a contract killing of Rs 25 lakh due to political rivalry.
Article Summary: The case was listed for pronouncement of judgment on Tuesday, but the special judge said he would need another two to three days to complete the order. Nimbalkar and Kazi were shot dead on June 3, 2006, while travelling from Mumbai to Osmanabad (now Dharashiv) Two assailants intercepted their car at Kalamboli in Navi Mumbai and opened fire.

Article Title: Kolhapur police tell devotees not to visit Mahalaxmi temple between 10.00 am to 11.00 am to avoid likely inconvenience due to Amit Shah’s visit
Article Text:
Kolhapur





: Police have appealed to devotees not to visit Mahalaxmi temple between 10 am and 11 am on Saturday to avoid inconvenience they might face due to the visit of union home minister Amit Shah The Kolhapur police issued a statement stating that the temple will not shut for the darshan. “During the visit of Shah, the temple will not shut for darshan, however to ensure safety arrangements, the devotees should visit the temple at other times,,” the statement released by the Kolhapur Police.Shah along with chief minister Devendra Fadnavis , deputy CM’s Eknath Shinde and Sunetra Pawar and several other state ministers from Maharashtra govt are going to attend the event of foundation laying of new Pradakshina Marg, and conservation of an old temple located in the premise of Mahalaxmi temple.Chandrakant Patil, senior BJP leader and state higher education minister said, “The Union home minister is a devotee of goddess Mahalaxmi. He has approved Rs 1500 cr from the Centre out of which Rs 900 crore will be spent on land acquisition and Rs 600 cr on beautification works. He wished to be at the temple on the first day of start of the works and accordingly he is arriving. We are also preparing for other works at the temple for which we are confident that union home minister will help get us funds. After the event, he will address rally at Kolhapur ‘Dhanyawad Amitbhai’ for sanctioning funds for Mahalaxmi temple and then at Ichalkaranji he will address rally on completion of 12 years of Prime Minister Narendra Modi .”
Article Summary: Kolhapur police issued a statement stating that the temple will not shut for the darshan. Amit Shah along with chief minister Devendra Fadnavis, deputy CM’s Eknath Shinde and Sunetra Pawar and several other state ministers from Maharashtra govt will attend the event.

Article Title: Teen who rammed SUV into woman before crashing into 6 vehicles in Navi Mumbai booked for drunk driving
Article Text:
Drunk teen SUV driver drags woman 50m, hits 6 parked vehicles

Watch CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash

NAVI MUMBAI: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver.The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside in Artist village, sector-8 on Friday.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur said, the accused SUV driver Vikram Kamble's medical test report received from NMMC hospital, Nerul confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol while driving.Subsequently, relevant section 185 Of Motor Vehicle Act that penalizes drunk driving were invoked against him and will be mentioned in the chargesheet that will be submitted in the court.The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees is recuperating at MGM hospital, CBD-Belapur and is out of danger, informed doctors.The accident took place in the residential locality of Artist village in Sector-8, CBD-Belapur around 5.30 pm on Friday. Police said the speeding SUV hit the woman from behind while she was walking close to the pavement and dragged her for nearly 50 metres before crashing into six vehicles parked along the roadside.Local residents rushed the injured woman to MGM Hospital, where she received prompt treatment. The incident was also captured on CCTV cameras installed in the area.Police said the SUV driver Vikram Gangadhar Kamble (19) was accompanied by his relative Akash Chandrakant Kamble (26), a private chauffeur. Both were detained by locals at the spot before the police arrived, following which an FIR was registered for rash and negligent driving and damage to property.
Article Summary: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver. The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside. The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees.

Article Title: Techie jumps to death from third floor; husband & mother-in-law booked
Article Text:
Whiff of harassment

Pune:





A 27-year-old software engineer jumped to death from the third floor of a residential building in the Manikbaug area on Thursday afternoon.The brother of the deceased stated in his complaint with the Sinhagad Road police that she ended her life because her husband and her mother-in-law harassed her. The same day, the police registered a case under Section 108 (abetment of suicide) of the BNS against the husband of the deceased, also a software engineer, his mother and other relatives.An officer from the Sinhagad Road police said the victim and her husband had met while working in a software company. They got married in Nov 2024.“According to the complainant, the victim’s husband, mother-in-law and other relatives used to harass her over petty issues and beat her up at times,” the officer said.He said, frustrated by the harassment, the woman jumped to death.
Article Summary: A 27-year-old software engineer jumped to death from the third floor of a residential building in the Manikbaug area on Thursday afternoon. The brother of the deceased stated in his complaint with the Sinhagad Road police that she ended her life because her husband and her mother-in-law harassed her. The police registered a case under Section 108 (abetment of suicide) of the BNS against the husband, his mother and other relatives.

Article Title: Talks with 6 Sena (UBT) MPs on for a year, some MLAs may switch sides too: Maval MP Barne
Article Text:
Shiv Sena's Maval MP Shrirang Barne

Pune:

TOI





Maval MP Shrirang Barne of Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena on Friday claimed that some MLAs from Uddhav Thackeray ’s party are keen on switching to their side. The much-speculated ‘operation tiger’ to engineer a second split in Uddhav’s party was executed earlier this week, but Barne claimed that discussions with the six MPs were on for a year.“People are getting information about the six MPs’ decisions now, but the process has been going on for a year. These MPs have been expressing their displeasure with the functioning of the Shiv Sena (UBT) leadership. UBT members were aware of the discussion,” Barne said.The Maval MP, who returned from Delhi a few days ago, said that some Shiv Sena (UBT) MLAs are planning to leave their party. “They and some elected representatives of local bodies from UBT are unhappy with the party’s functioning, and are looking for options. They complained that the leadership is not investing enough time in the party’s growth,” Barne said.His party colleague and state minister, Shambhuraj Desai, supported the Maval MP’s claim. Desai, speaking in Satara, said, “The restlessness among UBT MLAs is more than their MPs. I cannot speak officially, but those MLAs are thinking on the lines of ‘operation tiger’.”Uddhav Thackeray has called a meeting of party functionaries at his residence, Matoshree, on Saturday. Speaking to, Shiv Sena (UBT) MLA from Osmanabad Kailas Patil said, “I have got the message about Saturday’s meeting, but I do not know the agenda.I will attend along with some party functionaries from Dharashiv district. Members from other districts are also expected to be there.”Shiv Sena’s Mahayuti partner the BJP has said it has no role in the defection move and the opposition parties should introspect. “People have already lost faith in the opposition, but now elected representatives of these parties are moving away. The state has seen some parties and politicians performing well despite being in the opposition. However, the current opposition is weak and their leadership must find out why,” Jaykumar Gore, rural development minister and BJP member, said.
Article Summary: Maval MP Shrirang Barne of Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena claimed that some MLAs from Uddhav Thackeray ’s party are keen on switching to their side. The much- speculated ‘operation tiger’ to engineer a second split in the party was executed earlier this week. Barne claimed that discussions with six MPs were on for a year.

Article Title: Girish Mahajan hints at more defections in Sena (UBT)
Article Text:
Girish Mahajan hints at more defections in Sena (UBT)





NASHIK: Amid reports that six of the nine Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs may break away to form a separate group, and later merge with the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, senior BJP functionary and cabinet minister Girish Mahajan on Thursday said the development could trigger a wider exodus from the party, including corporators in the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC).Speaking to reporters in Nashik on Thursday, Mahajan claimed the reported move reflected deepening unrest within the Uddhav Thackeray-led faction and warned of further defections. "This is just the beginning. In the coming days, more leaders may take similar steps," he said.He suggested that dissatisfaction was not limited to MPs and could extend to MLAs and corporators. "People are fed up. MPs and MLAs are unhappy, and even corporators may take a call soon," he said, hinting at possible shifts in Mumbai's civic body.Taking a swipe at the party's ideological direction, Mahajan alleged that the Shiv Sena (UBT) had deviated from the legacy of its founder Balasaheb Thackeray after aligning with Congress and the NCP. "You may have secured the chief minister's post, but lost your core voter base," he said.Mahajan also criticised the party leadership, claiming it had failed to address growing discontent. "Workers and office-bearers were hurt.Instead of addressing their concerns, they were left on their own," he said.Targeting Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Raut, Mahajan blamed his remarks for fuelling internal unrest."Such remarks have not only upset the public but also party netas and workers," he said, adding in a swipe that Raut should "do Yoga to calm down" and may eventually join Congress.
Article Summary: Girish Mahajan warns of further defections in Shiv Sena (UBT) He says dissatisfaction is not limited to MPs and could extend to MLAs and corporators. He also criticised the party leadership for failing to address growing discontent.

Article Title: Near-total turnout in Nashik MLC polls, Jalgaon records 97.8% voting as Mahayuti eyes victory
Article Text:
Near-total turnout in Nashik MLC polls





NASHIK: Voting for the Maharashtra Legislative Council (MLC) elections in Nashik and Jalgaon concluded peacefully on Thursday, recording an impressive 99.8% turnout in Nashik and 97.8% in Jalgaon.In Nashik, 618 of the 619 eligible voters cast their ballots across nine centres, taking turnout to 99.8%, district collector and election officer Ayush Prasad said.Polling began slowly, with only about 9% voting recorded by noon, as many voters were reportedly out of the city, some travelling to Thane. The pace picked up sharply after 1pm, resulting in near-total participation by the close of voting.Mahayuti had fielded Shiv Sena's Narendra Darade from Nashik, while BJP rebel Gokul Gite withdrew from the contest after initially entering the fray as an independent.Expressing confidence, cabinet minister and senior BJP functionary Girish Mahajan said Mahayuti was poised for a landslide win."Mahayuti enjoys a clear majority with around 470 of the 619 voters in Nashik. Our candidate Narendra Darade will emerge victorious," he said, adding that the alliance would win all 17 MLC seats in the state and the opposition would "fail to secure a single seat."Polling in Nashik was held at centres in Nashik, Dindori, Igatpuri, Niphad, Yeola, Malegaon, Chandwad, Kalwan and Baglan.Except for Malegaon, which recorded 83 of 84 votes, all other centres saw 100% turnout.Officials said elaborate arrangements were made to ensure smooth and transparent polling, with adequate security and facilities as per Election Commission norms. Prasad thanked officials and voters for their participation and expressed satisfaction over the conduct of the election.In Jalgaon, 616 of the 630 eligible voters cast their votes, taking turnout to 97.8%. Polling was conducted peacefully across seven centres.The Jalgaon constituency witnessed a multi-cornered contest between BJP's Nandkishore Mahajan, MVA-backed Sharad Tayade and Shiv Sena rebel Reshma Kale. Counting of votes for both constituencies will take place on June 22.
Article Summary: Voting for the Maharashtra Legislative Council (MLC) elections in Nashik and Jalgaon concluded peacefully on Thursday. In Nashik, 618 of the 619 eligible voters cast their ballots across nine centres, taking turnout to 99.8%. Counting of votes for both constituencies will take place on June 22.

Article Title: DMK MP’s son, councillor booked for land fraud
Article Text: Chennai: North Chennai DMK MP Kalanidhi Veeraswamy’s son and three others including a DMK councillor have been booked by Anna Nagar police for alleged property fraud, criminal intimidation and caste-based abuse.The FIR named the suspects as Kalanidhi Veeraswamy’s son Siddharth , who is managing director of a private company, Rani Ravichandran, councillor of Ward 102 in Anna Nagar, Asaithambi, an assistant to the MP, and Adhinarayanan of Virudhunagar district.The case was registered on a complaint filed by Anbarasu, 67, of Karur district. The four have been booked under Sections 3(1)(r), 3(1)(s) and 3(2)(va) of the Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989, along with Sections 420 and 506(1) of Indian Penal Code.According to the complaint, the accused obtained a Power of Attorney deed from Anbarasu in connection with a property transaction but he failed to pay the agreed consideration amount. The complainant further stated that he was subjected to caste-based verbal abuse and criminal intimidation during the dispute.Police sources said the complaint was related to a property comprising 14 grounds of land and a building on Anna Salai. The complainant claimed that the property was mortgaged with a bank and that he was deceived in the transaction.
Article Summary: North Chennai DMK MP Kalanidhi Veeraswamy’s son and three others including a DMK councillor have been booked by Anna Nagar police. The case was registered on a complaint filed by Anbarasu, 67, of Karur district. The complainant claimed that he was subjected to caste-based verbal abuse and criminal intimidation during the dispute.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: HC notice to govt on plea to prevent colleges from seeking caste details
Article Text: HC notice to govt on plea to prevent colleges from seeking caste details
Article Summary: : HC notice to govt on plea to prevent colleges from seeking caste details. High Court: We will not allow colleges to seek caste details from students. High court: We have no power to ask for caste details of students. We will only allow them to provide information about their caste.

Article Title: Trial court was insensitive in ignoring mental illness of murder accused: HC
Article Text: Trial court was insensitive in ignoring mental illness of murder accused: HC
Article Summary: Trial court was insensitive in ignoring mental illness of murder accused, says HC. Trial court was 'insensitive' and 'ignored mental illness' of the accused, adds HC. The trial was adjourned until September 25. The verdict was delivered by a three-judge panel.

Article Title: As Hungary's Magyar joins EU summit, sidelined Orban meets with far-right allies in Brussels
Article Text: Fidesz party head and former prime minister Viktor Orban, center, speaks to the press as he arrives at the opposition Fidesz party's working congress meeting, in Budapest. (AP Photo)
Article Summary: Viktor Orban is the head of the opposition Fidesz party in Hungary. Orban was prime minister of Hungary from 1998 to 2008. He is now the leader of the anti- government Jobbik party, which is also in opposition.
